2605.02787.title: Static Analysis of Recursive SHACL
2605.02787.paper_summary: SHACL (Shapes Constraint Language) expresses constraints on RDF data by means of so-called shapes. Its central service is validation: verifying whether a data graph complies with a SHACL document. But so far, there are no static analysis services to compare documents. In this paper, we study the following problem: decide whether all graphs that validate one SHACL document also validate another. Unlike previous works that have considered the implication of shape expressions only, we consider documents comprising (recursive) shape definitions and targets. We show that implication (a.k.a. containment) is undecidable under the supported and the stable model semantics, even for the fragment that uses the description logic ALCIO for shape expressions. Under the well-founded semantics, in surprising contrast, it is decidable in single exponential time. Our key technical contribution is a translation of SHACL under the well-founded semantics into the full hybrid mu-calculus, revealing a novel link between well-founded models and a fixed point modal logic, and a worst-case optimal automata-based decision procedure.
2605.02787.paper_summary_zh: SHACL（形狀約束語言）通過所謂的形狀來表達對RDF數據的約束。<lb/>它的核心服務是驗證：檢查數據圖是否符合SHACL文檔。<lb/>但到目前為止，還沒有靜態分析服務來比較文檔。<lb/>在本文中，我們研究以下問題：決定是否所有驗證一個SHACL文檔的圖也驗證另一個文檔。<lb/>與之前僅考慮形狀表達式的含義的研究不同，我們考慮包含（遞歸）形狀定義和目標的文檔。<lb/>我們表明，在支持的和穩定模型語義下，含義（即包含）是不可判定的，即使對於使用描述邏輯ALCIO的形狀表達式的片段也是如此。<lb/>在良基語義下，令人驚訝的是，它在單指數時間內是可判定的。<lb/>我們的關鍵技術貢獻是將SHACL在良基語義下轉換為完整的混合μ-演算，揭示了良基模型與固定點模態邏輯之間的新聯繫，以及基於自動機的最壞情況最佳決策程序。
2605.02787.author: Anouk Oudshoorn et.al.
2605.02787.authors: Anouk Oudshoorn, Magdalena Ortiz, Mantas Simkus
